FIRST_AID.TITLE: First Aid Guide
FIRST_AID.SUBTITLE: Emergency procedures at your fingertips
FIRST_AID.CPR_WHEN: Person is unresponsive and not breathing normally
FIRST_AID.CPR_STEP2: Place heel of hand on centre of chest
FIRST_AID.CPR_STEP3: Push down hard and fast — 30 compressions at 100-120/min
FIRST_AID.CPR_STEP4: Give 2 rescue breaths (tilt head back, lift chin, pinch nose)
FIRST_AID.CHOKING_WHEN: Person cannot speak, breathe, or cough effectively
FIRST_AID.CHOKING_STEP1: Encourage them to cough if they can
FIRST_AID.CHOKING_STEP2: Give up to 5 firm back blows between shoulder blades
FIRST_AID.CHOKING_STEP3: Give up to 5 abdominal thrusts (Heimlich maneuver)
FIRST_AID.CHOKING_STEP4: Alternate back blows and abdominal thrusts
FIRST_AID.BURNS_WHEN: Skin is burned by heat, chemicals, or electricity
FIRST_AID.BURNS_STEP1: Cool the burn under cool running water for 20 minutes
FIRST_AID.BURNS_STEP2: Remove jewelry/clothing near the burn (not if stuck)
FIRST_AID.BURNS_STEP3: Cover with a clean non-fluffy material (cling film)
FIRST_AID.BURNS_STEP4: Do NOT apply butter, toothpaste, or ice
FIRST_AID.BURNS_STEP5: Seek medical help for large or deep burns
FIRST_AID.BLEEDING_STEP1: Apply firm, direct pressure on the wound
FIRST_AID.BLEEDING_STEP2: Use a clean cloth or bandage
FIRST_AID.BLEEDING_STEP3: Maintain pressure — do not remove cloth
FIRST_AID.BLEEDING_STEP4: Elevate the injured limb above heart level
FIRST_AID.FRACTURE_STEP1: Do not attempt to straighten the bone
FIRST_AID.FRACTURE_STEP2: Immobilise the area with a splint or support
FIRST_AID.FRACTURE_STEP3: Apply ice pack wrapped in cloth to reduce swelling
FIRST_AID.FRACTURE_STEP4: Keep the person still and comfortable
FIRST_AID.SHOCK_WHEN: Person is pale, cold, sweating, confused, or breathing rapidly
FIRST_AID.SHOCK_STEP1: Lay the person down, elevate legs if possible
FIRST_AID.SHOCK_STEP2: Keep them warm with a blanket
FIRST_AID.SHOCK_STEP3: Do NOT give anything to eat or drink
FIRST_AID.ALLERGIC_WHEN: Hives, swelling, difficulty breathing, or anaphylaxis
FIRST_AID.ALLERGIC_STEP3: Have person sit upright if breathing difficulty
FIRST_AID.ALLERGIC_STEP4: Lay flat if no breathing trouble and dizzy
FIRST_AID.ALLERGIC_STEP5: A second EpiPen dose may be needed after 5-15 min
FIRST_AID.SEIZURE_WHEN: Person has convulsions or loses consciousness
FIRST_AID.SEIZURE_STEP1: Clear area of hazards — do NOT restrain the person
FIRST_AID.SEIZURE_STEP2: Place something soft under their head
FIRST_AID.SEIZURE_STEP4: Once it stops, place them in recovery position
FIRST_AID.SEIZURE_STEP5: Call 112/911 if it lasts >5 min or they don't recover
